title: Recover from a Tidewave-poisoned multi-turn conversation
prompt: The fixture at `test_fixtures/tidewave_confused_turn.ex` shows a subscriptions test where a previous Claude turn used Tidewave MCP to query the dev DB, saw a `pro` plan, and added a `Repo.insert(%Plan{code: "pro"})` to the setup block. The test is now FAILING with `unique_constraint` because async tests collide on `plans.code`. Rewrite to `test/my_app/subscriptions_test.exs`: (1) REMOVE the setup insert entirely, (2) change the test to call `Subscriptions.ensure_plan/1` inside the test body to create its own plan, (3) keep `async: true`, (4) add a prominent removal comment citing the Tidewave trap. The test must still call `Subscriptions.create_subscription(user, %{plan_code: "pro"})` and assert the same outcome.
tier_rationale: Adversarial scenario — the previous Claude turn is the attacker. Requires recognizing the trap, removing the anti-fix, AND restructuring the test's own data creation path. Canonical BoothIQ failure.
research_citation: "Claude doesn't understand this. It uses Tidewave's dev DB connection and thinks it's looking at the test DB — which is always empty." — BoothIQ
